title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: `ServiceSpecDefaults` is missing `IPFamilyPolicy` normalization, and `EnsureService` doesn't preserve `IPFamilies` from the existing spec. This causes a nil-vs-defaulted mismatch on every reconcile, producing spurious Service updates in a loop. The fix: default `IPFamilyPolicy` to `SingleStack` in `ServiceSpecDefaults`, and copy `IPFamilies` from the existing Service in `EnsureService` (like `ClusterIP` already is).

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Fully addressed: (1) Added IPFamilyPolicy static defaulting to SingleStack in ServiceSpecDefaults (normalize/service.go:25-28), matching the reviewer's suggested code exactly. (2) Added IPFamilies preservation from existing.Spec in EnsureService (deployment/deployment.go:94), placed alongside ClusterIP/ClusterIPs preservation as suggested. (3) Updated both unit tests in service_test.go: TestServiceSpecDefaults now asserts IPFamilyPolicy is set to SingleStack (lines 33-34), and TestServiceSpecDefaults_preservesExplicitValues now sets IPFamilyPolicy to PreferDualStack and asserts it is not overwritten (lines 45, 50, 61).

resolution_summary: Both parts of the review feedback were fully addressed. IPFamilyPolicy defaulting to SingleStack was added to ServiceSpecDefaults in normalize/service.go (lines 25-28), and IPFamilies preservation from the existing spec was added to EnsureService in deployment/deployment.go (line 94), mirroring the existing ClusterIP/ClusterIPs preservation pattern. The test file service_test.go was also updated to cover the new IPFamilyPolicy normalization in both the defaults test and the preserves-explicit-values test.
